div.p: through all.1 But if Joseph Smith says there are Gods many and Lords many,2 they cry, “Away with him! Crucify him! Crucify him!”3

Mankind verily say that the Scriptures are with them.4 Search the Scriptures,5 for they testify of things that these apostates would gravely pronounce blasphemy.6 Paul, if Joseph Smith is a blasphemer, you are. I say there are Gods many and Lords many, but to us only one,7 and we are to be in subjection to that one,8 and no man can limit the bounds or the eternal existence of eternal time.9 Hath he beheld the eternal world,10 and is he authorized to say that there is only one God?11 He makes himself a fool if he thinks or says so, and there is an end of his career or progress in knowledge. He cannot obtain all knowledge, for he has sealed up the gate to it.

Some say I do not interpret the Scripture12 the same as they do. They say it means the heathen’s gods.13 Paul says there are Gods many and Lords many;14 and that makes a plurality of Gods, in spite of the whims of all men. Without a revelation, I am not going to give them the knowledge of the God of heaven.15 You know and I testify that Paul had no allusion to the heathen gods.16 I have it from God, and get over it if you can. I have a witness of the Holy Ghost,17 and a testimony that Paul had no allusion to the heathen gods in the text. I will show from the Hebrew Bible that I am correct, and the first word18 shows a plurality of Gods; and I want the apostates and learned men to come here and prove to the contrary, if they can. An unlearned boy must give you a little Hebrew. Berosheit baurau Eloheim ait aushamayeen vehau auraits, rendered by King James’ translators, “In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.”19 I want to analyze the word Berosheit. Rosh, the head; Sheit, a grammatical termination; the Baith was not originally put there when the inspired man wrote it, but it has been since added by an old Jew. Baurau signifies to bring forth; Eloheim is from the word Eloi,20 God, in the singular number; and by adding the word heim, it renders it Gods. It read first, “In the beginning the head of the Gods brought forth the Gods,”21 or, as others have translated it, “The head of the Gods called the Gods together.” I want to show a little learning as well as other fools.
